Off Calendar Mar-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MCKESSON CORPORATION, JOHNSON & JOHNSON, MCNEIL CONSUMER HEALTHCARE, and, WAL-MART
STORES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION regarding manufacturing defect, design defect, breach of warranty, and punitive damages.. Appearances: Daniel K. Balaban, Esq. of Balaban & Spielberger LLP, (310) 566-1750; appearing for Plaintiff, and opposing party; Andrew J. Spielberger, Esq. of Balaban & Spielberger LLP, (310) 566-1750; appearing for plaintiffs, Thomas, Diana, and, Gary Gaines, and The Estate of Thomas B. Gaines, and moving party; Alan VanBelde, Esq. of Greene Broillet & Wheeler LLP (310) 576-1200, appearing for Plaintiffs, Thomas, Diana, and, Gary Gaines, and The Estate of Thomas B. Gaines, and opposing party; Thomas W. Pulliam Jr., Esq. of DrinkerBiddle&Reath LLP, (415) 591-7570; appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Objections to sur-reply papers sustained. Request for judicial notice of Wyeth vs. Levine case is granted. Motion for Summary adjudication for failure to adequately warn is denied. Defendants failed to shift burden of proof. (See Wyeth vs. Levine.) Motion for Summary Adjudication for Breach of Warranty is denied. Privity established through reasonings in Gottsdanker vs. Cutter Laboratories, 182 Cal.App. 2nd, 602. Motion for Summary Adjudication for design defect, manufacturing defect, and punitive damages is granted. Plaintiff's raise no triable issue of fact that the Children's Motrin was manufactured defectively or designed defectively. No clear and convincing evidence of oppression, fraud, or malice. (CC section 3294(a).The Court addes: Plaintiffs' Evidentiary objections are all overruled. Defendants' evidentiary objections are filed (and endorsed filed copies are given to Counsel before the hearing.) Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/VC) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
